source_documents: https://www.fatf-gafi.org (Guidance on Politically Exposed Persons, Rec 12/22)
category: AML - politically exposed person (PEP) and corruption-proceeds risk
risk_signals: Transaction values or wealth inconsistent with the PEP's known legitimate income
Funds routed through family members, associates, or shell entities to distance the PEP
Flows timed around public procurement, contracts, or tenders the PEP could influence
analyst_reasoning: PEPs carry heightened corruption and bribery risk because their public position can be monetised. The laundering signature is unexplained wealth relative to office, layered through proxies to obscure the connection, and often linked to state contracts - so source-of-wealth verification is central.
investigation_steps: Establish and verify the PEP's source of wealth and source of funds
Map close associates, family, and controlled entities used as conduits
Correlate significant flows with procurement, tenders, or contracts within the PEP's influence
decision: Apply enhanced due diligence and senior-management sign-off, and file an STR where wealth or flows cannot be explained.
outcome: Corruption proceeds laundered via proxies are hard to attribute; source-of-wealth verification is the decisive control.
lessons_learned: Unexplained wealth versus known income is the central PEP red flag
Family, associates, and shell entities are the standard PEP layering conduits
PEP relationships require enhanced due diligence and source-of-wealth verification by policy
